goal: Port structural.hotspots + structural.orphans to view-native; rename orphans file/function to match the rule name (dead_code.py → orphans.py, run_unreferenced → run_orphans)

summary.title: Port structural.hotspots + structural.orphans to view-native
summary.what: Both cross-cutting rules drop legacy_v2_shim. Two new Structure view methods (hotspots, orphans) take root + params, return rich records (FileHotspot, OrphanCandidate) from slop/structure/records.py. Compute logic in private modules slop/structure/_hotspots.py (aggregate per-file CCX from Structure.cyclomatic + git_log_numstat + quadrant classification) and _orphans.py (enumerate top-level callables + class-like scopes, grep_kernel word-boundary refs, heuristic confidence). Renamed slop/linter/rules/dead_code.py -> orphans.py and function run_unreferenced -> run_orphans for consistency with the rule name (structural.orphans). Retired _compose/hotspots.py (549 lines) and _compose/prune.py (370 lines). 505 tests pass (+4 new orphans tests). View-native rule count: 13 -> 15. Shim-wrapped count: 14 -> 12.
summary.why: Cross-cutting rules needed external systems (git, ripgrep) but the AST work they were doing — per-file CCX aggregation for hotspots, symbol enumeration for orphans — duplicated what Structure already provides. The port replaces the duplication with view consumption and isolates the external-system access (git_log_numstat, grep_kernel) to small helpers. Also fixes the dead_code/orphans/unreferenced naming inconsistency that had three names for one rule across file/function/registry layers.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.hotspots + structural.orphans to view-native; rename orphans file/function to match the rule name (dead_code.py → orphans.py, run_unreferenced → run_orphans)

decisions.chose: Pass root explicitly to view methods that need external systems, rather than carrying it on Structure. Symmetric with coupling(scope, known_classes) — view methods take corpus-wide context as parameters.
Private compute modules (_hotspots.py / _orphans.py) instead of growing view.py past 1100 lines. View methods stay as thin dispatchers.
Rename file/function to match published rule name (structural.orphans). User chose this over renaming the rule to structural.dead_code.
Orphans enumerates top-level callables + class-like scopes only. Methods/nested-functions/lambdas skipped: name-based ref counting on them is too noisy (matches every property access).
Retire both legacy kernels in this intent rather than deferring. The view methods replicate the kernels' main logic; keeping them dormant would invite drift.
